schools.summary: Gill: the whole body of the people came thither from Gilgal, where the camp and tabernacle had been; the land was subdued before them.

themes: the whole congregation... assembled together at Shiloh, and set up the tabernacle... And the land was subdued
God taking up his dwelling in the midst of his people
this is my rest for ever: here will I dwell (Ps. 132:14)

schools.summary: Gill: these were Benjamin, Simeon, Zebulun, Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Dan.
Keil & Delitzsch: the seven tribes not yet provided for.

themes: there remained among the children of Israel seven tribes, which had not yet received their inheritance
God's gift ready but unclaimed through the people's slowness
all things are yours (1 Cor. 3:21)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua said this to those of the seven tribes.
Keil & Delitzsch: the rebuke, How long are ye slack to possess the land?

themes: How long are ye slack to go to possess the land, which the LORD God of your fathers hath given you?
the fault of being slow to lay hold of God's gift
be not slothful, but followers of them who inherit the promises (Heb. 6:12)

schools.summary: Gill: three men for each of the seven tribes, twenty-one in all; some think out of all the tribes; they should go through the land and describe it.

themes: Give out from among you three men for each tribe... they shall... go through the land, and describe it
possessing God's gift with diligence and order
let all things be done decently and in order (1 Cor. 14:40)

schools.summary: Gill: divided into seven parts according to the seven tribes not yet settled.
Clarke: Judah abiding on the south, Joseph on the north.

themes: they shall divide it into seven parts: Judah shall abide... on the south, and the house of Joseph... on the north
God providing a place for each of his people
in my Father's house are many mansions (John 14:2)

schools.summary: Gill: they were first to describe the land, then divide it into seven parts, and bring the description; Joshua would cast lots before the LORD.

themes: bring the description hither to me, that I may cast lots for you here before the LORD our God
human diligence joined to divine appointment
the lot is cast into the lap; but the whole disposing thereof is of the LORD (Prov. 16:33)

schools.summary: Gill: the Levites needed send no men to measure land, having no part, which is why the land was divided into seven parts only.
Clarke: the priesthood of the LORD their inheritance.

themes: the Levites have no part among you; for the priesthood of the LORD is their inheritance
those set apart to serve God finding their portion in him
the LORD is my portion, saith my soul (Lam. 3:24)

schools.summary: Gill: the seven tribes took Joshua's advice, chose three men each, and presented them to him for their commission; they were to walk through the land and describe it.

themes: the men arose, and went away... Go and walk through the land, and describe it, and come again to me
the readiness that should attend God's work
whatsoever thy hand findeth to do, do it with thy might (Eccl. 9:10)

schools.summary: Gill: they passed through undisturbed, the fear of Israel still on the inhabitants and the providence of God restraining them; described it by cities in a book.

themes: the men went and passed through the land, and described it by cities into seven parts in a book
God protecting his servants and restraining their enemies
the angel of the LORD encampeth round about them that fear him (Ps. 34:7)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua cast lots for the seven tribes at Shiloh, as he had for the two and a half at Gilgal; divided the land according to their divisions.

themes: Joshua cast lots for them in Shiloh before the LORD: and there Joshua divided the land
the inheritance referred wholly to God's disposal
in all thy ways acknowledge him, and he shall direct thy paths (Prov. 3:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the first of the seven lots; a small lot, 'little Benjamin,' but the land very pleasant and fruitful, between Judah and Joseph.

themes: the lot of the tribe of... Benjamin came up... between the children of Judah and the children of Joseph
God honouring little Benjamin with a goodly, central portion
God hath chosen the things which are not... that no flesh should glory (1 Cor. 1:28-29)

schools.summary: Gill: Jordan was the eastern boundary, and the northern border ran from east to west, described like the lot of Joseph.
Keil & Delitzsch: the northern border of Benjamin.

themes: their border on the north side was from Jordan... by... Jericho... westward
God's care equal toward the least of his people
not one sparrow shall fall without your Father (Matt. 10:29)

schools.summary: Gill: from Beth-aven the western border began and went on to Luz.
Keil & Delitzsch: Luz, which is Bethel, southward.

themes: the border went over from thence toward Luz, to the side of Luz, which is Bethel
Bethel, the house of God, within Benjamin's heritage
ye shall see... the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man (John 1:51)

schools.summary: Gill: the border drawn from Beth-horon.
Wesley: the Israelites changed the name Kirjath-baal, to blot out the remembrance of Baal.

themes: the goings out thereof were at Kirjathbaal, which is Kirjathjearim... this was the west quarter
the idol's name blotted out from the inheritance
I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth (Hos. 2:17)

schools.summary: Gill: the south quarter began where the western quarter ended, at Kirjath-jearim; went out to the well of waters of Nephtoah.

themes: the south quarter was from the end of Kirjathjearim... to the well of waters of Nephtoah
the heritage watered and supplied by God's provision
ye shall draw water out of the wells of salvation (Isa. 12:3)

schools.summary: Gill: here the measure was from west to east, where in Judah's border it went up.
Keil & Delitzsch: the border by the valley of the son of Hinnom.

themes: the border came down... before the valley of the son of Hinnom... in the valley of the giants
the valley of later abomination within the heritage
the wicked shall be turned into hell (Ps. 9:17)

schools.summary: Gill: the border turned northward, looking from west to east.
Clarke: En-shemesh, 'the fountain of the sun,' a proof of the idolatrous nature of the ancient inhabitants.

themes: was drawn from the north, and went forth to Enshemesh... toward Geliloth
En-shemesh, 'the sun's fountain,' a witness to the old idolatry
lest... when thou seest the sun... thou shouldest worship them (Deut. 4:19)

schools.summary: Gill: this is the same with Beth-arabah, so called in the Greek version; the border went down unto Arabah.

themes: passed along toward the side over against Arabah northward, and went down unto Arabah
the heritage taking in plain and hill alike
the earth is the LORD's, and the fulness thereof (Ps. 24:1)

schools.summary: Gill: Beth-hoglah was included in the lot of Benjamin.
Clarke: the north bay of the Salt Sea.

themes: the outgoings of the border were at the north bay of the salt sea... this was the south coast
God's gift clearly fixed within sure bounds
thou hast set a bound that they may not pass over (Ps. 104:9)

schools.summary: Gill: Benjamin had Jordan on the east, Dan on the west, Judah on the south, and Joseph on the north; this was the inheritance of Benjamin.

themes: And Jordan was the border of it on the east side. This was the inheritance of the children of Benjamin
God giving a portion entire and well-bounded
the LORD is the portion of mine inheritance... I have a goodly heritage (Ps. 16:5)

schools.summary: Gill: introduces the cities of Benjamin.
Keil & Delitzsch: Jericho, Beth-hoglah, and the valley of Keziz as the first towns.

themes: the cities of the tribe of the children of Benjamin... were Jericho, and Bethhoglah, and the valley of Keziz
Jericho, the first conquest, now an inheritance
he brought them to the border of his sanctuary (Ps. 78:54)

schools.summary: Gill: Beth-arabah, of which he speaks elsewhere.
Keil & Delitzsch: Zemaraim, probably the ruins of es Sumrah, and Bethel.

themes: And Betharabah, and Zemaraim, and Bethel
Bethel, the house of God, place of holy memory and later apostasy
this is none other but the house of God... the gate of heaven (Gen. 28:17)

schools.summary: Gill: of the first two of these we read nowhere else.
Keil & Delitzsch: Avvim (i.e., ruins) is unknown, Phara preserved in the ruins of Fara.

themes: And Avim, and Parah, and Ophrah
the works of men decay while God's appointment stands
the grass withereth... but the word of our God shall stand for ever (Isa. 40:8)

schools.summary: Gill: of the first two of these no mention is made elsewhere.
Keil & Delitzsch: Chephar-haammonai, Ophni, and Gaba, twelve cities.

themes: And Chepharhaammonai, and Ophni, and Gaba; twelve cities with their villages
God's provision precise and full, none uncounted
he telleth the number of the stars; he calleth them all by their names (Ps. 147:4)

schools.summary: Gill: Gibeon is the place from whence the Gibeonites came, who deceived Joshua; a village four miles from Bethel.
Keil & Delitzsch: Gibeon, Ramah, and Beeroth.

themes: Gibeon, and Ramah, and Beeroth
Gibeon, the deceivers' city, become a seat of God's worship
the strangers... that join themselves to the LORD, to serve him (Isa. 56:6)

schools.summary: Gill: frequent mention is made of Mizpeh in Scripture, about eight miles from Gibeah, near Ramah.
Keil & Delitzsch: Mizpeh, Chephirah, and Mozah.

themes: And Mizpeh, and Chephirah, and Mozah
Mizpeh, the later scene of Samuel's gathering and Israel's rescue
Ebenezer... Hitherto hath the LORD helped us (1 Sam. 7:12)

schools.summary: Gill: of these cities there is no mention made elsewhere.
Keil & Delitzsch: Rekem, Irpeel, and Taralah.

themes: And Rekem, and Irpeel, and Taralah
God's gifts not depending on man's remembrance
the LORD knoweth them that are his (2 Tim. 2:19)

schools.summary: Gill: Zelah was the burying-place of Saul and his family.
Clarke: Zelah, the burying-place of Saul, Jonathan, and the family of Kish; Jebusi, which is Jerusalem.

themes: Jebusi, which is Jerusalem, Gibeath... fourteen cities... This is the inheritance of the children of Benjamin
little Benjamin holding the future city of God and the first king's city
the LORD hath chosen Zion... for his habitation (Ps. 132:13)
